title: Tanarius boutonioides
intro: Tanarius boutonioides, also known as Tanacetum, is a flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae, characterized by its bright yellow or white flowers and strong, pungent scent. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to reduce fever, relieve pain, and soothe skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Tanarius boutonioides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and volatile oils, including camphor, borneol, and limonene, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Tanarius boutonioides is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or essential oil, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, flu, and digestive problems.
